one_time_description: Capital assets destroyed in the Lytton Creek wildfire on June 30, 2021; five band buildings and 39 houses destroyed
economic_notes: Lytton Creek wildfire on June 30, 2021 destroyed band office, five band buildings, 39 houses on three reserves, and 90% of Village of Lytton structures. Significant rebuild and infrastructure restoration underway. Three GBEs: Kumcheen Enterprises Ltd., Lytton Development Corp., and Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP.
notes: Audit is qualified due to the June 30, 2021 Lytton Creek wildfire which destroyed the band office and all financial records. Auditors could not verify completeness of donation revenue, miscellaneous revenue of $1,570,564, or supporting documentation for expenses. Five segments fully consolidated (Operating Fund, Social Housing Fund, Capital Fund, Trust Fund, Enterprise Fund) plus three GBEs on modified equity basis. ISC funding jumped dramatically from $11.6M to $37.1M largely due to wildfire recovery funding. Revenue line 'Other' of $5,488,991 and Band Programs 'other revenue' of $10,536,759 likely include wildfire relief/insurance proceeds. Deferred revenue current shown as negative in revenue lines as it reduces recognized revenue per segmented statements. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($37,128,953) + FNHA ($1,542,490) + CMHC ($166,940) + BC Province ($2,211,421) + Department of Justice ($132,582) + FNES Committee ($352,265) + BC Hydro ($70,259) + Legal Aid ($27,819) + Land Management ($10,000) + Works program ($1,520) + Gaming ($446,221) - deferred revenue adjustment ($1,928,057) approximated at sum of identified government sources before deferred; annual_debt_service is principal repayments: $23,801 + $132,037 = $155,838.
